stem:
x | f(x)
−4 | 0
−19/5 | 1
−18/5 | 2

For the linear function f, the table shows three values of x and their corresponding values of f(x). If h(x) = f(x) − 13, which equation defines h?
explanation: Between x = −4 and x = −19/5 the input rises by 1/5 while f rises by 1, so the slope is 5. Using f(−4) = 0 gives f(x) = 5x + 20. Then h(x) = f(x) − 13 = 5x + 20 − 13 = 5x + 7.
